categories.label: Candlestick Patterns
categories.description: Single, double, and sequence candle behaviors used for directional context.

categories.label: Market Structure
categories.description: Price delivery logic using swings, breaks, and character shifts.

categories.label: Liquidity Concepts
categories.description: Where stops cluster, how sweeps occur, and how reactions form.

categories.label: Timeframes
categories.description: Top-down alignment and context from higher to lower timeframe execution.

categories.label: Entry Models (ICT Concepts)
categories.description: Institutional execution models like OB, FVG, breaker and mitigation.

categories.label: Trading Abbreviations
categories.description: A searchable glossary linked to practical chart examples.

categories.label: Risk Management
categories.description: Position protection, expectancy, and capital sustainability.

concepts.title: Doji
concepts.definition: Open and close are nearly equal, signaling indecision at that price zone.
concepts.idealContext: At HTF support/resistance before confirmation candle.
concepts.beginnerMistake: Entering immediately without waiting for confirmation.
concepts.timeframeRelevance: Cleaner on H1+; noisy on very low timeframes.
concepts.practicalUsage: Use as a warning candle, then wait for structure confirmation.

concepts.title: Hammer
concepts.definition: Small body with long lower wick showing aggressive rejection.
concepts.idealContext: Discount zone after liquidity sweep into support.
concepts.beginnerMistake: Ignoring surrounding trend and trading every hammer.
concepts.timeframeRelevance: Higher timeframe hammers carry stronger intent.
concepts.practicalUsage: Use with BOS/CHoCH confirmation for continuation entries.

concepts.title: Shooting Star
concepts.definition: Small body with long upper wick showing upside rejection.
concepts.idealContext: Premium zone after buy-side liquidity run.
concepts.beginnerMistake: Shorting in the middle of range without context.
concepts.timeframeRelevance: Best when aligned with HTF bearish bias.
concepts.practicalUsage: Pair with liquidity sweep + CHoCH for precision shorts.

concepts.title: Bullish Engulfing
concepts.definition: Bullish candle body fully engulfs prior bearish body.
concepts.idealContext: After discount rejection near demand.
concepts.beginnerMistake: Ignoring liquidity targets overhead.
concepts.timeframeRelevance: Strong when formed after sweep of sell-side liquidity.
concepts.practicalUsage: Use as LTF confirmation inside HTF bullish narrative.

concepts.title: Bearish Engulfing
concepts.definition: Bearish candle body fully engulfs prior bullish body.
concepts.idealContext: After premium rejection near supply.
concepts.beginnerMistake: Not confirming with structure shift.
concepts.timeframeRelevance: Higher quality when paired with BSL sweep.
concepts.practicalUsage: Use for LTF entry after bearish CHoCH.

concepts.title: Inside Bar
concepts.definition: Current candle range is fully within previous candle range.
concepts.idealContext: Consolidation before range expansion.
concepts.beginnerMistake: Entering before break confirmation.
concepts.practicalUsage: Use breakout direction with HTF bias filter.

concepts.title: Pin Bar
concepts.definition: Long wick and small body signaling rejection.
concepts.idealContext: At key PD arrays with liquidity run.
concepts.beginnerMistake: Reading every pin bar as reversal.
concepts.practicalUsage: Treat as intent candle, then confirm with structure.

concepts.title: Morning Star
concepts.definition: Three-candle bullish reversal sequence from bearish momentum.
concepts.idealContext: After SSL sweep into HTF discount.
concepts.beginnerMistake: Ignoring whether middle candle is true pause/rejection.
concepts.timeframeRelevance: Less noisy and stronger on higher timeframes.
concepts.practicalUsage: Use as transition signal from distribution to accumulation.

concepts.title: Evening Star
concepts.definition: Three-candle bearish reversal sequence from bullish momentum.
concepts.idealContext: After BSL sweep into HTF premium.
concepts.beginnerMistake: Shorting without confirmation close below structure.
concepts.timeframeRelevance: High signal quality with HTF confluence.
concepts.practicalUsage: Use as transition signal into markdown.

concepts.title: Three White Soldiers
concepts.definition: Three consecutive strong bullish candles with progressive closes.
concepts.idealContext: After CHoCH into displacement move.
concepts.beginnerMistake: Chasing late after move is exhausted.
concepts.timeframeRelevance: Best when aligns with fresh BOS.
concepts.practicalUsage: Use as momentum confirmation, then wait retrace.

concepts.title: Three Black Crows
concepts.definition: Three consecutive strong bearish candles with progressive closes.
concepts.idealContext: After CHoCH into displacement down.
concepts.beginnerMistake: Entering without identifying next liquidity target.
concepts.timeframeRelevance: Stronger with fresh bearish structure break.
concepts.practicalUsage: Use momentum confirmation and trade pullback entries.

concepts.title: Tweezer Top
concepts.definition: Two candles rejecting near-identical highs.
concepts.idealContext: At EQH/BSL area before downside displacement.
concepts.beginnerMistake: Assuming reversal without liquidity context.
concepts.practicalUsage: Treat as rejection clue before structure confirmation.

concepts.title: Tweezer Bottom
concepts.definition: Two candles rejecting near-identical lows.
concepts.idealContext: At EQL/SSL area before upside displacement.
concepts.beginnerMistake: Buying before shift in order flow.
concepts.practicalUsage: Use with CHoCH and displacement for entries.

concepts.title: Order Block Candle
concepts.definition: Final opposite candle before displacement that causes BOS.
concepts.idealContext: After sweep and shift in market structure.
concepts.beginnerMistake: Marking every opposite candle as OB.
concepts.timeframeRelevance: Use HTF narrative, execute on LTF.
concepts.practicalUsage: Refine entry at OB with risk-defined invalidation.

concepts.title: Mitigation Candle
concepts.definition: Return candle revisiting imbalance/OB for order mitigation.
concepts.idealContext: After displacement and before continuation.
concepts.beginnerMistake: Entering before reaction confirms mitigation.
concepts.practicalUsage: Enter after mitigation tap plus confirmation candle.

concepts.title: Displacement Candle
concepts.definition: Large impulsive candle breaking structure and creating imbalance.
concepts.idealContext: Immediately after liquidity sweep.
concepts.beginnerMistake: Chasing the impulse instead of waiting retrace.
concepts.timeframeRelevance: Key signal of smart money intent.
concepts.practicalUsage: Anchor model on displacement source and inefficiency.

concepts.title: Inducement Candle
concepts.definition: Candle that attracts breakout participation before reversal.
concepts.idealContext: Just before major liquidity run.
concepts.beginnerMistake: Treating inducement as true breakout.
concepts.practicalUsage: Use to identify trap leg before entry leg.

concepts.title: Breaker Formation
concepts.definition: Failed OB that flips role and becomes continuation zone.
concepts.idealContext: After structure break through prior OB.
concepts.beginnerMistake: Confusing breaker with random retest.
concepts.practicalUsage: Use breaker retest as continuation entry model.

concepts.title: Break of Structure (BOS)
concepts.definition: Price closes beyond prior swing level in trend direction.
concepts.idealContext: After pullback continuation.
concepts.beginnerMistake: Using wick break without close confirmation.
concepts.timeframeRelevance: Higher TF BOS carries stronger directional bias.
concepts.practicalUsage: Use BOS to validate continuation rather than reversal.

concepts.title: Change of Character (CHoCH)
concepts.definition: First opposing break indicating possible trend transition.
concepts.idealContext: After liquidity grab against prior trend.
concepts.beginnerMistake: Calling CHoCH too early inside consolidation.
concepts.timeframeRelevance: LTF CHoCH is stronger when HTF has reached PD extremes.
concepts.practicalUsage: Use as trigger to look for new directional model.

concepts.title: Market Structure Shift (MSS)
concepts.definition: Structure transition confirmed by displacement through protected swing.
concepts.idealContext: Post-sweep with strong impulse.
concepts.beginnerMistake: Mistaking minor micro breaks for true shift.
concepts.practicalUsage: Use MSS to confirm entry model direction.

concepts.title: Higher High / Higher Low (HH/HL)
concepts.definition: Bullish sequence where highs and lows progressively rise.
concepts.idealContext: Trending market with pullback continuation.
concepts.beginnerMistake: Buying highs instead of pullbacks to HL.
concepts.practicalUsage: Plan entries at HL around discounts.

concepts.title: Lower High / Lower Low (LH/LL)
concepts.definition: Bearish sequence where highs and lows progressively fall.
concepts.idealContext: Trending downside with corrective pullbacks.
concepts.beginnerMistake: Counter-trend longs without confirmation.
concepts.practicalUsage: Plan entries at LH in premium area.

concepts.title: Equal Highs (EQH)
concepts.definition: Two or more highs at similar levels attracting stop liquidity.
concepts.idealContext: Before downside expansion after BSL run.
concepts.beginnerMistake: Assuming level cannot be swept.
concepts.practicalUsage: Expect sweep through EQH before distribution.

concepts.title: Equal Lows (EQL)
concepts.definition: Two or more lows at similar levels attracting stop liquidity.
concepts.idealContext: Before upside expansion after SSL run.
concepts.beginnerMistake: Buying before sweep completes.
concepts.practicalUsage: Wait for sweep + reclaim before entry.

concepts.title: Sell-Side Liquidity (SSL)
concepts.definition: Stops resting below recent lows.
concepts.idealContext: Below range lows before reversal setup.
concepts.beginnerMistake: Buying before stops are taken.
concepts.practicalUsage: Use SSL sweep as precursor to long model.

concepts.title: Buy-Side Liquidity (BSL)
concepts.definition: Stops resting above recent highs.
concepts.idealContext: Above range highs before reversal setup.
concepts.beginnerMistake: Shorting before highs are raided.
concepts.practicalUsage: Use BSL sweep then wait for CHoCH.

concepts.title: Liquidity Sweep
concepts.definition: Temporary break through liquidity pool followed by rejection.
concepts.idealContext: At session highs/lows near HTF PD arrays.
concepts.beginnerMistake: Confusing sweep with true breakout continuation.
concepts.practicalUsage: Wait for sweep, then structure shift confirmation.

concepts.title: Inducement
concepts.definition: Engineered mini-structure attracting entries before the true move.
concepts.idealContext: Before displacement away from trap side.
concepts.beginnerMistake: Entering on inducement break.
concepts.practicalUsage: Map inducement leg and avoid being liquidity.

concepts.title: Stop Hunt
concepts.definition: Intentional move to trigger clustered stops before reversal.
concepts.idealContext: Around EQH/EQL and session extremes.
concepts.beginnerMistake: Placing obvious stops at equal levels.
concepts.practicalUsage: Use stop hunts as entry context, not signals alone.

concepts.title: HTF Bias
concepts.definition: Higher timeframe directional narrative used as trade filter.
concepts.idealContext: Before selecting lower timeframe entries.
concepts.beginnerMistake: Executing LTF setups against HTF direction.
concepts.practicalUsage: Anchor plan with HTF bias and key levels.

concepts.title: LTF Entry
concepts.definition: Lower timeframe trigger aligned with HTF narrative.
concepts.idealContext: After HTF zone touch and LTF confirmation.
concepts.beginnerMistake: Entering purely from HTF level without LTF trigger.
concepts.practicalUsage: Use CHoCH/MSS in LTF to execute HTF idea.

concepts.title: Multi-Timeframe Alignment
concepts.definition: Combining HTF context and LTF execution for consistency.
concepts.idealContext: When daily narrative and intraday model agree.
concepts.beginnerMistake: Mixing contradictory timeframe narratives.
concepts.practicalUsage: Use top-down flow: D1 > H4 > H1 > M15/M5.

concepts.title: Scalp / Intraday / Swing Profiles
concepts.definition: Execution style changes based on holding duration and timeframe stack.
concepts.idealContext: Before session open, define profile.
concepts.beginnerMistake: Using scalp management on swing setups.
concepts.timeframeRelevance: Defines stop placement and target horizon.
concepts.practicalUsage: Choose profile and adapt expectations.

concepts.title: Order Block (OB)
concepts.definition: Last opposing candle prior to displacement causing structure break.
concepts.idealContext: Post-sweep and displacement with inefficiency.
concepts.beginnerMistake: Drawing oversized OB zones without invalidation.
concepts.timeframeRelevance: Best as LTF execution inside HTF zones.
concepts.practicalUsage: Enter on OB revisit with confirmation.

concepts.title: Fair Value Gap (FVG)
concepts.definition: Three-candle imbalance where middle candle leaves a delivery gap.
concepts.idealContext: After displacement for continuation or mitigation.
concepts.beginnerMistake: Taking every FVG without narrative context.
concepts.timeframeRelevance: Higher quality when nested in HTF narrative.
concepts.practicalUsage: Use as rebalance zone for precise entries.

concepts.title: Inverse FVG (IFVG)
concepts.definition: Invalidated FVG that flips directional role.
concepts.idealContext: After failed continuation and structure shift.
concepts.beginnerMistake: Treating IFVG as normal FVG continuation.
concepts.practicalUsage: Use flipped inefficiency as reaction zone.

concepts.title: Breaker
concepts.definition: Failed swing/OB level repurposed after structure violation.
concepts.idealContext: After confirmed MSS and displacement.
concepts.beginnerMistake: Confusing with random support/resistance flips.
concepts.practicalUsage: Execute on breaker retest with invalidation beyond source.

concepts.title: Mitigation
concepts.definition: Price returns to unfilled institutional area before continuation.
concepts.idealContext: Post-displacement retrace.
concepts.beginnerMistake: Expecting immediate move without confirmation.
concepts.practicalUsage: Wait for mitigation touch plus LTF trigger.

concepts.title: Premium / Discount
concepts.definition: Price location relative to dealing range midpoint.
concepts.idealContext: Range or swing leg planning.
concepts.beginnerMistake: Ignoring range definition quality.
concepts.timeframeRelevance: Improves directional entries at value extremes.
concepts.practicalUsage: Favor longs in discount, shorts in premium.

concepts.title: Optimal Trade Entry (OTE)
concepts.definition: Preferred retracement zone used for efficient entries.
concepts.idealContext: After displacement and clear range anchor.
concepts.beginnerMistake: Forcing OTE in choppy conditions.
concepts.timeframeRelevance: Works best with confluence and narrative alignment.
concepts.practicalUsage: Use OTE as one confluence factor, not standalone.

concepts.title: PD Array
concepts.definition: Set of delivery arrays such as OB, FVG, breaker used for execution.
concepts.idealContext: After narrative and liquidity mapping.
concepts.beginnerMistake: Overloading chart with every possible array.
concepts.practicalUsage: Choose one primary and one secondary array.

concepts.title: Risk to Reward (RR)
concepts.definition: Ratio between potential loss and potential gain.
concepts.idealContext: Before order placement.
concepts.beginnerMistake: Taking low RR trades repeatedly.
concepts.practicalUsage: Filter out setups below your plan threshold.

concepts.title: Stop Loss (SL)
concepts.definition: Invalidation level where trade idea is proven wrong.
concepts.idealContext: Beyond structural invalidation.
concepts.beginnerMistake: Placing SL at obvious liquidity pools.
concepts.timeframeRelevance: Position sizing depends on SL distance.
concepts.practicalUsage: Place SL where your narrative fails.

concepts.title: Take Profit (TP)
concepts.definition: Planned profit-taking level aligned with liquidity targets.
concepts.idealContext: At opposing liquidity or structure objective.
concepts.beginnerMistake: Moving TP randomly due to emotions.
concepts.practicalUsage: Scale partials at intermediate targets.

concepts.title: Break Even (BE)
concepts.definition: Move stop to entry after sufficient confirmation.
concepts.idealContext: After first target or structural confirmation.
concepts.beginnerMistake: Moving to BE too early and getting clipped.
concepts.timeframeRelevance: Improves downside control when used correctly.
concepts.practicalUsage: Use a rules-based BE trigger, not emotion.

concepts.title: Drawdown
concepts.definition: Peak-to-trough equity decline during a trading period.
concepts.idealContext: Weekly and monthly performance review.
concepts.beginnerMistake: Increasing size to recover losses quickly.
concepts.practicalUsage: Set max drawdown stop rules for the week.

steps.label: Step 1: Identify Structure
steps.instruction: Click the primary structure leg first.

steps.label: Step 2: Locate Liquidity
steps.instruction: Select the EQH/EQL pool likely to be targeted.

steps.label: Step 3: Wait for Sweep
steps.instruction: Click the sweep zone once liquidity is taken.

timeframeProfiles.label: Scalping
timeframeProfiles.biasNote: Focus on session liquidity windows and tighter invalidation.

timeframeProfiles.label: Intraday
timeframeProfiles.entryExample: H4 bias + M15 MSS + FVG rebalance
timeframeProfiles.biasNote: Use NY/London session overlap and same-day targets.

timeframeProfiles.label: Swing
timeframeProfiles.biasNote: Hold through broader structure legs with wider stops.
